top_signals.title: Gold Steadies as Traders Assess Rates Path After US-Iran Clashes
top_signals.summary: Gold steadied after the US and Iran ramped up attacks over the weekend, as traders assess the likelihood of the Federal Reserve raising interest rates to contain inflation.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Europe Was Supposed to Run Out of Jet Fuel by June. It Didn’t
top_signals.summary: Early warnings surrounding the Strait of Hormuz suggested that European airports and fuel markets could face physical shortages by the beginning of summer. Those shortages largely failed to materialise, revealing both the adaptability of global energy markets and their persistent tendency to price worst-case scenarios long before they occur. When the Strait of Hormuz was effectively closed at the end of February, the first forecasts were dramatic. The disruption affected a route that had carried nearly 20 million barrels per day of crude oil and…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: CNBC Daily Open: Spain takes the World Cup, oil takes the market spotlight
top_signals.summary: Spain took home their first World Cup since 2010 after a 1-0 victory over 10 man Argentina.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Bowman, Opening Remarks on Sound Practices for Artificial Intelligence
top_signals.summary: Speech At the Financial Stability Board Virtual Outreach Event
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Venezuela’s Official Death Toll From Twin Quakes Rises to 5,208
top_signals.summary: Venezuela’s official death toll from the twin earthquakes that struck the country on June 24 rose to 5,208, according to the latest figures released on Sunday by National Assembly President Jorge Rodríguez.
